title: What is an Exception? 💥
description: Why apps crash when things go wrong.

slides.title: The App Stopped Responding 📱
slides.content:
Have you ever used an app on your phone, tapped a button, and the app instantly closed itself and returned you to the home screen?

That wasn't the app closing. That was the app **crashing** because it encountered an unhandled Java Exception.

slides.title: The Root Cause 💥
slides.content:
An **Exception** is an unwanted or unexpected event that occurs during execution, disrupting the normal flow of the program.

For example, what happens if we try to do math by dividing by Zero? The universe breaks.

quizData.question: What specifically causes an `IndexOutOfBoundsException`?

options.text: Trying to ask for index 5 when the array only holds 3 items.
Putting the wrong type of data in a variable.

quizData.explanation: Asking for an address that doesn't exist throws an `IndexOutOfBoundsException` and kills the app.

slides.title: Is it the developer's fault? 🤔
slides.content:
Sometimes exceptions are your fault (like dividing by zero). 

But often, they are out of your control! What if your app tries to fetch a high score from a database, but the User's phone has no internet connection? 

The code will throw an Exception, and the app will crash...

slides.title: The Solution 🧯
slides.content:
...BUT, it doesn't have to crash!

Java gives developers tools to intercept these explosions in mid-air, allowing us to safely alert the user ("Please check your connection") instead of killing the app.

Let's learn how in the next lesson.
